content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

August 10, 1994


Kay Paietta, Legal Assistant
Gibson, Dunn & Crutcher
333 South Grand Avenue
Los Angeles, CA 90071-3197

Re: Your Request for Confirmation
of Telephone Advice
Our File No. A-94-233

Dear Ms. Paietta:

You have requested confirmation of telephone advice provided
to you concerning the lobbying disclosure provisions of the
Political Reform Act.¹

Except as stated below, your letter accurately reflects the
advice given to you. It is my understanding that Gibson, Dunn &
Crutcher registered as a lobbying firm as of June 2, 1994. So
long as the firm did not qualify as lobbying firm until that date
it is not obligated to report lobbying activities for any period
prior to the quarter covering April 1, 1994 through June 30, 1994.

The provisions of Regulation 18614(a)(2) and (a)(3) apply to
payments made from a lobbyist employer to a lobbying firm and
would not apply to an entity which receives payment for lobbying
related activities prior to qualifying as a lobbying firm.

Your understanding that payments received for general legal
advice and litigation or pre-litigation services are not
reportable by a lobbying firm is correct.

Regulation 18616 provides that lobbyist employers and persons
spending $5,000 or more to influence legislative or administrative
action need not report compensation or other payments for services
which are solely secretarial, clerical or manual or limited solely
to the compilation of data and statistics. This regulation does
not apply to lobbying firms. Hence, the entire amount a lobbying firm

_____________
1 Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations
Section 18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title
2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.


428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento, CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

Padetta, Kay
File No. A-94-233
Page 2


firm receives for the purpose of influencing legislative or
administrative action is reportable by the lobbying firm even
though a portion of the payment may be used to pay the salary of
its employees who perform solely secretarial, clerical or manual
services or who provide services which are limited solely to the
compilation of data and statistics.

If you have additional questions, please contact me at (916)
322-5662.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        Steven G. Churchwell
                                        General Counsel

                                        [signature]
                                        By: Wayne P. Imberi
                                            Political Reform Consultant

sections.question_synthetic: What are the lobbying disclosure obligations for Gibson, Dunn & Crutcher following their registration as a lobbying firm in June 1994, and what payments must be reported under the Political Reform Act?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The firm is not obligated to report lobbying activities prior to the quarter covering April 1-June 30, 1994. Payments for general legal advice and litigation services are not reportable. All amounts received by a lobbying firm for influencing legislative or administrative action are reportable, even if portions are used for secretarial or clerical work, as the exemption in Regulation 18616 does not apply to lobbying firms.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Letter format with no explicit QUESTION/CONCLUSION section headers. Content appears to confirm prior telephone advice. Multiple regulatory citations provided. Requestor is legal assistant at Gibson, Dunn & Crutcher seeking confirmation of prior informal guidance.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: What are the lobbying disclosure obligations for Gibson, Dunn & Crutcher following their registration as a lobbying firm in June 1994, and what payments must be reported under the Political Reform Act?

CONCLUSION: The firm is not obligated to report lobbying activities prior to the quarter covering April 1-June 30, 1994. Payments for general legal advice and litigation services are not reportable. All amounts received by a lobbying firm for influencing legislative or administrative action are reportable, even if portions are used for secretarial or clerical work, as the exemption in Regulation 18616 does not apply to lobbying firms.

embedding.summary: FPPC confirmation letter addressing lobbying disclosure obligations for Gibson, Dunn & Crutcher following their June 2, 1994 registration as a lobbying firm, clarifying reporting requirements and what payments must be disclosed.
